visa_policy.notes: The Bahamas does not operate a visa-on-arrival or e-visa system for general travellers. The authoritative visa-free list is published by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (mofa.gov.bs) at the not-need-visas page. Indian nationals are NOT unconditionally visa-free; they require a valid US, UK, Canada, or Schengen visa or PR status (covered in conditional_access). All nationalities not on the visa-free list must obtain a Bahamian visa in advance through a Bahamas consulate, embassy, or Ministry of Foreign Affairs. US citizens may stay up to 8 months; most South American and Central American nationals up to 3 months; most other visa-free nationals up to 3 months unless a bilateral agreement specifies otherwise. Visitors must present valid passport (minimum 6 months validity), return ticket, proof of funds, and a Disembarkation/Embarkation card. The BEATS (Bahamas Extended Access Travel Stay) digital nomad program launched in 2020 was suspended in early 2022. The Special Permit program listed on immigration.gov.bs is specifically for persons on the Restricted List (previously deported/removed) - it is not a travel or nomad program. An Immigration (Amendment) Bill was introduced in 2024 (2024-0045). The MOFA not-need-visas list contains some apparent data quality issues (individual UAE emirates listed separately, 'Scotland' listed as a separate entry, 'Span' as a typo for Spain, 'Galapagos Islands' as a separate entry) - these have been consolidated into their sovereign state entries.

conditional_access.conditions: Alien residents of the United States who, upon arrival, are in possession of United States Alien Registration Cards for visits not exceeding thirty (30) days. Must also hold a valid national passport.

Landed immigrants in possession of Canadian Immigration Record Form 1000, or holders of a Canadian Permanent Resident Card, may enter without a visa for visits not exceeding 30 days. Must hold a valid national passport. Passport and PR card expiry must not be less than 3 months after departure (direct travel) or 6 months if transiting through other countries.

Indian Nationals who have a valid United States of America visa do not require a Bahamas visa. Visas will be issued on arrival. Must present the valid US visa together with valid passport to Immigration at time of entry. Indian nationals without a qualifying credential require a Bahamas visa in advance.

Indian Nationals who have a valid United Kingdom visa do not require a Bahamas visa. Visas will be issued on arrival. Must present the valid UK visa together with valid passport to Immigration at time of entry.

Indian Nationals who have a valid Canadian visa do not require a Bahamas visa. Visas will be issued on arrival. Must present the valid Canadian visa together with valid passport to Immigration at time of entry.

Indian Nationals who have a valid Schengen visa do not require a Bahamas visa. Visas will be issued on arrival. Must present the valid Schengen visa together with valid passport to Immigration at time of entry.

Indian Nationals who are permanent residents of the United States of America do not require a Bahamas visa. Must present proof of Permanent Residency status together with valid passport to Immigration at time of entry.

Indian Nationals who are permanent residents of the United Kingdom do not require a Bahamas visa. Must present proof of Permanent Residency status together with valid passport to Immigration at time of entry.
Indian Nationals who are permanent residents of Canada do not require a Bahamas visa. Must present proof of Permanent Residency status together with valid passport to Immigration at time of entry.

Indian Nationals who are permanent residents of a Schengen country do not require a Bahamas visa. Must present proof of Permanent Residency status together with valid passport to Immigration at time of entry.

Indian nationals who hold diplomatic or official passports do not require a visa to enter The Bahamas. Bilateral Visa Waiver Agreement signed 29 November 2018 between the Commonwealth of The Bahamas and the Republic of India for holders of diplomatic and official passports.
Effective August 2019, holders of diplomatic, official and service passports from Chile do not need visas to enter The Bahamas.

cbi.notes: The Bahamas does not offer a direct citizenship-by-investment program. Citizenship is obtained through naturalization only after a qualifying period as a permanent resident (10 years of legal permanent resident status including 12 months immediately preceding application, plus 6 years prior residency). No investment-to-citizenship fast track is offered. The official immigration website and bahamas.gov.bs confirm only permanent residence by investment is available, not citizenship by investment. Sources checked: https://www.immigration.gov.bs/applying-to-stay/applying-for-citizenship/, https://www.bahamas.gov.bs/citizenship-and-naturalization.

programs.name: Economic Permanent Residence
programs.notes: Minimum investment of USD $1.0 million either through real estate or purchase of Zero Coupon Bonds from the Central Bank of The Bahamas. Both investment types must be held for a minimum of 10 years. The current immigration.gov.bs page does not specify an expedited/speedy consideration threshold for BSD $1.5 million real estate - this detail could not be re-confirmed on the live page and has been moved to gaps. Processing fee: $200 non-refundable. Requires medical certificate (within 30 days), police certificate (within 6 months, covering 5 years of residence), passport photographs, birth certificate, two character references. Path to citizenship requires 10 years as permanent resident (including 12 months immediately before application) plus 6 years prior residency.

programs.name: Bahamas Extended Access Travel Stay (BEATS)
programs.eligibility: Remote workers and students working/studying for employers/institutions outside the Bahamas
programs.notes: Launched October 2020, the BEATS program allowed remote workers and students to live and work remotely in the Bahamas for up to 12 months (renewable twice for up to 3 years total). No minimum income requirement, but applicants needed income from outside the Bahamas. The program was suspended in early 2022. No replacement program has been confirmed on official sources as active. Referenced in official press release at https://www.immigration.gov.bs/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/BMOTA-BEATS-_Press_Release-_FINAL_10.29-converted.pdf. Note: The Special Permit listed on immigration.gov.bs is NOT a nomad/travel visa - it is exclusively for persons previously deported or on the Restricted List.

programs.name: Expedited Permanent Residence for High-Value Real Estate Investment
programs.eligibility: Investors purchasing residential real estate valued at BSD $1.5 million or more
programs.processing_time: Speedy/expedited consideration - could not be re-confirmed on live immigration.gov.bs page as of 2026 re-verification
programs.notes: Previously documented that persons purchasing a residence for BSD $1.5 million or more receive speedy consideration for their permanent residence applications. The live immigration.gov.bs page (re-checked 2026) only states the USD $1.0 million minimum without mentioning a BSD $1.5 million expedited tier. This item could not be re-confirmed and source_official has been set to false. May still exist as internal policy.

agreements: CARICOM (member but does not participate in CSME free movement)

validation_notes: Re-verified against live mofa.gov.bs/consular-services/not-need-visas-enter-bahamas/, mofa.gov.bs/consular-services/needs-visas-enter-bahamas/, mofa.gov.bs/visa-facilitation-indian-nationals/, and immigration.gov.bs/entry-requirements/ on 2026-06-29. All existing visa_free entries confirmed present on live MOFA page. No additions or removals to visa_policy lists required. MOFA not-need-visas page still includes the same non-passport-issuing territory names (French Southern Territories, South Georgia and South Sandwich, Azores, Tahiti, Galapagos Island, Pitcairn, Northern Mariana, Scotland, individual UAE emirates) which remain correctly consolidated into their sovereign state entries. Indian nationals VoA facilitation (US/UK/Canada/Schengen visa holders) confirmed still active on mofa.gov.bs/visa-facilitation-indian-nationals/. Economic PR minimum investment of USD $1.0 million re-confirmed on live immigration.gov.bs/residence/permanent-residence/ - no BSD $1.5M expedited tier mentioned. Needs-visas list re-confirmed: Bosnia still contradictorily appears on both lists; Bosnia kept in visa_free per affirmative not-need-visas page as more specific. Ghana confirmed visa-free on MOFA page (despite immigration.gov.bs legacy text still citing Commonwealth exception for Ghana - MOFA not-need-visas page is the authoritative and more recent source). No structural changes made on this run.

Re-verified against live mofa.gov.bs/consular-services/not-need-visas-enter-bahamas/, mofa.gov.bs/consular-services/needs-visas-enter-bahamas/, mofa.gov.bs/visa-facilitation-indian-nationals/, and immigration.gov.bs/entry-requirements/ on 2026-06-27. All existing visa_free entries confirmed present on live MOFA page. No additions or removals required to visa_policy lists. Two new territory names found on live MOFA page ('French Southern Territories' and 'South Georgia and South Sandwich') - not added as they are not passport-issuing nationalities (French overseas territory and UK overseas territory respectively; covered by France/FRA and UK/GBR already in visa_free). Indian nationals VoA facilitation (US/UK/Canada/Schengen visa holders) confirmed still active on mofa.gov.bs/visa-facilitation-indian-nationals/. Economic PR minimum investment of USD $1.0 million confirmed on live immigration.gov.bs/residence/permanent-residence/ - no BSD 1.5M expedited tier mentioned.
Re-verified against live mofa.gov.bs/consular-services/not-need-visas-enter-bahamas/ and mofa.gov.bs/consular-services/needs-visas-enter-bahamas/ on 2026-06-14.
ADDED Ghana to visa_free: Previous record incorrectly excluded Ghana citing it as a Commonwealth exception. The live MOFA not-need-visas page explicitly lists Ghana as visa-exempt.
ADDED Kosovo (XKX) to visa_free: Present on live MOFA not-need-visas page, absent from previous record.
ADDED Benin (BEN) to visa_free: Present on live MOFA not-need-visas page, absent from previous record.
ADDED Rwanda (RWA) to visa_free: Present on live MOFA not-need-visas page, absent from previous record.
ADDED Wallis and Futuna to visa_free: Present on live MOFA not-need-visas page, absent from previous record.
REMOVED duplicate 'Saint Marten' entry (was a duplicate/variant of Saint Martin, which is retained).
REMOVED 'Azores' as a separate visa_free entry: Azores is an autonomous region of Portugal, not a separate passport-issuing nationality. Portuguese nationals are already covered under Portugal (PRT).
REMOVED 'Pitcairn' as a separate visa_free entry: Pitcairn is a British Overseas Territory and does not issue passports separately; UK (GBR) covers Pitcairn Islanders.
REMOVED 'Norfolk Islands' as a separate visa_free entry: Australian territory, not a separate passport-issuing nationality.
REMOVED 'Northern Mariana Islands' as a separate visa_free entry: US territory; US nationals covered under USA (USN/USA).
REMOVED 'Galapagos Islands' as a separate visa_free entry: Part of Ecuador (ECU); Galapagos Islands residents travel on Ecuadorian passports.
REMOVED 'Tahiti' as a separate visa_free entry: Part of French Polynesia; already retained as 'French Polynesia' which is on the MOFA list.
REMOVED 'Netherlands Antilles' as a separate visa_free entry: The Netherlands Antilles was dissolved in 2010; constituent islands (Curacao, Sint Maarten, etc.) are listed separately.

REMOVED the catch-all 'Most British Commonwealth nations (excluding Pakistan, Cameroon, Mozambique, India, Nigeria, Ghana)' entry: This entry was ambiguous and inaccurate. Individual countries are now enumerated from the MOFA source. Ghana is now visa-free (confirmed); India remains in conditional_access only.
UPDATED source_url for all visa_free entries to authoritative MOFA not-need-visas page where previously only the immigration.gov.bs/entry-requirements/before-your-arrival/ URL was cited.
UPDATED fast_track: Expedited BSD $1.5M PR tier marked source_official=false as it could not be re-confirmed on live immigration.gov.bs permanent-residence page.
UPDATED 'Macedonia' to 'North Macedonia' (MKD) to use current official country name.
UPDATED 'Swaziland' note to reflect current country name 'Eswatini' while preserving the MOFA source text.

CLARIFIED: India is NOT unconditionally visa-free - it appears on the MOFA not-need-visas list ONLY because holders of US/UK/Canada/Schengen visas or PR cards do not need a visa. India remains in conditional_access and is not added to visa_policy.visa_free.
CONFIRMED: No e-visa or visa-on-arrival system for general travellers. No active digital nomad visa as of 2026 re-verification.
gaps: Exact max_stay_days not confirmed for all visa-free nationalities - general rule is up to 8 months for most, 3 months for South/Central American nationals; specific bilateral agreements not all available verbatim.
Dual citizenship policy for Bahamian citizenship not explicitly stated on official pages reviewed.
The BEATS digital nomad program suspension (early 2022) is based on third-party reports; no official government announcement of suspension found on immigration.gov.bs.
No e-visa or VOA for general travellers - confirmed absent on official sources. Indian national VoA facilitation is the only conditional exception.
bahamas.gov.bs main pages return HTTP 403 and could not be directly verified; data sourced from immigration.gov.bs and mofa.gov.bs which are the official sites.
Max stay duration for India conditional_access entries (VoA and PR-based) not specified on official mofa.gov.bs pages.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) card-specific rule found on any official Bahamas government source; not recorded.
The BSD $1.5 million expedited PR tier for real estate investors could not be re-confirmed on the live immigration.gov.bs page as of 2026; may exist as internal policy or may have been updated.
Processing time for Economic Permanent Residence not stated on official page for the standard USD $1M investment route.

Some MOFA not-need-visas list entries appear to be data quality issues on the official page itself: individual UAE emirates listed (Dubai, Fujairah, Ras Al Khaimah, Sharjah, Umm Al Quwain) alongside UAE; 'Scotland' listed as separate entry; 'Spain' listed as 'Span' (typo confirmed corrected); 'Galapagos Island' as separate entry; 'French Southern Territories' and 'South Georgia and South Sandwich' listed as separate entries (non-passport-issuing territories, covered by France/FRA and UK/GBR). These have been consolidated but the underlying MOFA list data quality is noted.

Countries explicitly listed on MOFA needs-visas page include: Afghanistan, Albania, Algeria, Angola, Belarus, Bhutan, Bosnia (contradicted by not-need-visas list), Burkina Faso, Burundi, Cambodia, Cameroon, Central African Republic, Chad, Comoros, Congo, Cote d'Ivoire, Cuba, Djibouti, Dominican Republic, Egypt, Equatorial Guinea, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Gabon, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Haiti, Indonesia, Iran, Iraq, Jordan, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Laos, Lebanon, Liberia, Libya, Macau, Madagascar, Mauritania, Micronesia, Mongolia, Montenegro, Morocco, Mozambique, Myanmar, Nepal, Niger, Nigeria, North Korea, Pakistan, Palau, Palestine, Philippines, Saudi Arabia, Senegal, Serbia, Somalia, Sudan, Syria, Taiwan, Tajikistan, Thailand, Timor-Leste, Togo, Tunisia, Turkmenistan, Ukraine, Uzbekistan, Vietnam, Yemen, Zaire. Bosnia appears on both lists - a contradiction on official MOFA pages.
No evidence found of conditional entry based on holding Australian, Japanese, GCC, or UAE credentials on official sources (beyond UAE nationals themselves being visa-free).

outbound_official.notes: Official MOFA page last amended 2014-02-18. Page includes disclaimer that Bahamians should verify with destination embassies as requirements change without notice. Dominican Republic listed as 'tourist card on arrival'; Mexico listed as 'travel card on arrival' - both treated as visa_free per the MOFA page grouping. Palau listed as 'issued upon arrival'. US entry noted with caveat: police record required; visa required for cruises/transiting. Norfolk Island and Netherlands Antilles appear on the list despite being non-sovereign territories. Some entries (Galapagos Islands) in the original list are sub-national regions and have been omitted. The list does not distinguish e-visa or ETA categories - only 'visa not required' vs 'visa required'.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals not exempt from visa requirements to enter the Bahamas for tourism, leisure, or visiting family. Nationals of approximately 80+ countries must obtain this visa in advance through a Bahamas embassy, consulate, or the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
visa_types.notes: Issued through Bahamas embassies and consulates abroad, or via 40+ consular offices of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs worldwide. Applicants from visa-required countries include Afghanistan, Algeria, Cuba, Egypt, Haiti, India (without qualifying credentials), Iran, Nigeria, Pakistan, Philippines, Russia, Syria, Ukraine, Vietnam and others. Visitors must hold a valid return/onward ticket and proof of sufficient funds. Standard max stay up to 8 months subject to immigration officer discretion. Work is strictly prohibited on a visitor visa.

visa_types.name: Business Visitor Visa / BH-1B Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to enter the Bahamas for short-term business activities such as meetings, negotiations, audits, or inspections without engaging in gainful employment.
visa_types.notes: The BH-1B visa fee is BSD $3,000 per the official Immigration Fee Scale. Business visitors are not permitted to engage in any form of gainful employment or work. Short-term business activities such as board meetings and corporate inspections may qualify. Separate from the Short Term Work Permit which authorizes actual work.

visa_types.name: Work Permit (Work Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Authorizes non-Bahamian nationals to engage in gainful employment with a specific employer in the Bahamas. Issued annually in nine fee scales based on occupation level.
visa_types.notes: Annual work permits are issued on a fee scale of 1-9 based on occupation: Scale 1 (executives/directors) BSD $16,275; Scale 2 (professionals/managers) BSD $13,125; Scale 3 (technical/healthcare) BSD $9,450; Scale 4 (skilled trades/supervisors) BSD $7,500; Scale 4A (consultants/physicians) BSD $6,250; Scale 5 (trades/hospitality) BSD $6,000; Scale 6 (instructors/artists) BSD $5,000; Scale 7 (clerical/entry-level) BSD $4,000; Scale 8 (general workers) BSD $2,000; Scale 9 (farm laborers) BSD $500. Religious/charitable organizations and passenger ship workers: Nil. Expedited processing available for Scales 7-9 (14 business days) at BSD $400; Scales 1-6 at 10% surcharge. Applications via online portal at epermits.immigration.gov.bs. Permit is employer-specific. Renewal available.

visa_types.name: Short Term Work Permit
visa_types.purpose: Authorizes non-Bahamian nationals to perform work in the Bahamas for a period of 1 to 90 days, typically for emergency or one-time professional assignments.
visa_types.notes: For 1-30 days: BSD $200 non-refundable processing fee. For 31-90 days: additional documentation required including police certificate and medical certificate. Designated for emergency situations and intended as a one-time application. Examples include aircraft mechanics, corporate auditors, board meeting attendees, and disaster preparedness personnel. Certain professions (medical, legal, accounting, entertainment, veterinary) require authorization letters from relevant professional councils. Applicants should not be in the Bahamas during processing. Online application via epermits.immigration.gov.bs.

visa_types.name: Government Contract Worker Permit
visa_types.purpose: Authorizes foreign nationals employed under government contracts to reside and work in the Bahamas for the duration of their contract.
visa_types.notes: Specific fee, duration, and requirements are not published on the immigration website. Applications via online portal at epermits.immigration.gov.bs. Contact: (242) 225-5337 or (242) 322-7530-5.

visa_types.name: Permit to Reside (Student)
visa_types.purpose: Authorizes non-Bahamian nationals to reside in the Bahamas for the purpose of attending school, college, or university, or as dependents of persons legally authorized to reside in the country.
visa_types.notes: Annual permit fee BSD $300 for full-time students/trainees per the Immigration Fee Scale. Processing fee BSD $200 non-refundable. Processing time 8-12 weeks after complete submission. Applicants should not be in the Bahamas during processing. Requirements include: letter of request to Director of Immigration, completed notarized Form 1, medical certificate (within 30 days), police certificate (within 6 months covering 5 years), two passport photos, valid passport copy, sponsor passport copy, two character references, financial reference, annual permit questionnaire, and school enrollment documentation. Renewal available.

visa_types.name: Resident Spouse Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-Bahamian nationals married to Bahamian citizens to legally reside and work in the Bahamas.
visa_types.notes: Annual permit fee BSD $300 (or BSD $1,500 for a 5-year spouse permit option). Non-refundable processing fee BSD $200. For marriages of less than 5 years at application. After 5 years of marriage, the non-Bahamian spouse may apply for renewal, permanent residence, or citizenship. Applicants should not be in the Bahamas during initial processing. Requirements: completed notarized Form 1A, medical certificate (within 30 days), police certificate (within 6 months), passport photos, valid passports for both spouses, certified marriage certificate, birth certificates for children under 18. All foreign documents must be apostille-certified or legalized. Renewal available.

visa_types.name: Permit to Reside (Dependent)
visa_types.purpose: Authorizes dependent children (under 18) of persons legally authorized to reside in the Bahamas to reside in the country.
visa_types.notes: Annual dependent permit fee BSD $300 per the Immigration Fee Scale. Processing fee BSD $200 non-refundable. Same application as Permit to Reside (Student). Applies to spouses or children under 18 of persons legally authorized to reside. Processing time 8-12 weeks.

visa_types.name: Permit to Reside (Retired Person)
visa_types.purpose: Allows retired foreign nationals with at least 20 years of work history to reside in the Bahamas without engaging in gainful employment.
visa_types.notes: Annual permit fee BSD $250 for retired persons with 20 years work history per the Immigration Fee Scale. Non-refundable processing fee BSD $200. Requirements include financial reference or CPA statement demonstrating ability to self-sustain. Not authorized for gainful employment.

visa_types.name: Economic Permanent Residence Certificate
visa_types.purpose: Grants lifelong permanent resident status to foreign nationals making a qualifying investment of at least USD $1 million in Bahamian real estate or Zero Coupon Bonds, allowing the holder to reside and work.
visa_types.notes: Minimum investment of USD $1.0 million in real estate OR purchase of Zero Coupon Bonds from the Central Bank of the Bahamas, with mandatory 10-year holding period. Certificate fee: BSD $25,000 with gainful occupation rights; BSD $20,000 without. Certificate is issued for the lifetime of the holder unless revoked. Processing fee BSD $200 non-refundable. Requirements include medical certificate (within 30 days), police certificate (within 6 months, covering 5 years), two passport photos, passport copies, birth certificate, two character references. Path to citizenship requires 10 years as permanent resident (including 12 months immediately before application) plus 6 years of prior residency.

visa_types.name: General Permanent Residence Certificate
visa_types.purpose: Grants lifelong permanent resident status to foreign nationals with long-term qualifying residence or professional service in the Bahamas, allowing the holder to reside and work.
visa_types.notes: Standard applicant fee BSD $15,000. Reduced fees apply for: persons with 10-20 years residence on a work permit (scales 4-7) at BSD $10,000; 10-20 years as teacher/nurse/healthcare/government worker at BSD $3,000; 20+ years on higher professional work permits at BSD $1,000-$2,000; previously endorsed spouses (widowed) or children on parent certificate at BSD $3,000; spouses of permanent residents at BSD $1,000-$5,000; ministerial/religious workers with 20+ years at BSD $1,000. Certificate is for the lifetime of the holder unless revoked. Eligibility categories include spouses of Bahamians (after 5 years of marriage), children born abroad to Bahamian mothers, long-serving teachers/nurses/police/prison officers, ministers of religion, doctors, and legal work permit holders of 20+ consecutive years.

visa_types.name: Home Owner Identification Card
visa_types.purpose: Provides a residence identification card for foreign nationals who own a habitable residence in the Bahamas and maintain a primary residence in their home country, issued under the International Persons Land Holding Act.
visa_types.notes: Non-refundable processing fee BSD $200. Applicants must own a habitable residence in the Bahamas AND maintain a primary residence outside the Bahamas. Issued under International Persons Land Holding Act – Chapter 140. Requirements: completed notarized application form with BSD $10 postage stamp, letter of request to Director, medical certificate (within 30 days), police certificate (within 6 months), two passport photos, valid passport copy (minimum 2 months validity), current real property tax payment proof, registered deed of conveyance for Bahamian home, International Landholding Certificate, marriage/birth certificates if applicable, Good Standing certificate, evidence of primary home outside Bahamas (insurance, mortgage, or tax documents). Applicants should not be in the Bahamas during processing. Same form applies for renewals.

visa_types.name: Resident Belonger Permit
visa_types.purpose: Authorizes persons born in the Bahamas to non-Bahamian parents, or persons born abroad to a Bahamian mother or father, who have not automatically acquired Bahamian citizenship to reside in the Bahamas.
visa_types.notes: Annual permit fee BSD $300 for all Resident Belonger categories per the Immigration Fee Scale. Applies to persons with ancestral ties to the Bahamas who have not acquired Bahamian citizenship. Applications via online portal at epermits.immigration.gov.bs.

visa_types.name: Diplomat Identification Card
visa_types.purpose: Provides identification and status documentation for accredited diplomatic personnel and their recognized dependents residing in the Bahamas.
visa_types.notes: Issued to accredited diplomatic personnel. Specific fees and requirements not published on the immigration website. Contact: (242) 225-5337 or (242) 322-7530-5.

visa_types.name: Special Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals whose names appear on the Bahamas Restricted List (previously deported or removed) to enter the Bahamas for a limited period, subject to approval by the Director of Immigration.
visa_types.notes: Non-refundable fee BSD $200. Maximum 28 days at the Director's discretion. Exclusively for persons on the Restricted List - NOT a general travel or nomad visa. Requirements: cover letter to Director of Immigration, police certificate (within 6 months, covering 5 years) for applicants aged 14+, two passport photos (white background, within 6 months), valid passport copy with 6+ months remaining validity, two written references (one from employer). Permit must be carried at all times while in the Bahamas and surrendered upon departure.

visa_types.name: Re-Entry Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-Bahamian permanent residents or authorized residents to re-enter the Bahamas after travel abroad.
visa_types.notes: Specific fees and requirements not published on the immigration website. Applications via online portal at epermits.immigration.gov.bs. Contact: (242) 225-5337 or (242) 322-7530-5.
